  ---
title: "sampling_analysis_SP_C_06_18_25"
format: html
---
###Load Libraries

```{r load libraries}
library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)
library(janitor)
library(stringr)
library(dplyr)
library(leaflet)
library(sf)
library(patchwork)
library(purrr)
library(lme4)
library(lmerTest)
library(dbscan)
library(broom)
library(scales)
library(car)
library(kableExtra)
```


### Load + Clean Data:

```{r data pull, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# -----------------------------
# Load + clean to analysis-ready
# -----------------------------

nutrients <- c("lbc_1","lb_ceq","p_h_2","ca","k","mg","mn","p","zn")

lime_clean <- read_csv("../data_do_not_push/2021 Liming Study Soil Data Compiled (lbs. A).csv") %>%
  clean_names() %>%
  separate(sample, into = c("type", "sample_site"), sep = ",\\s*", remove = TRUE) %>%
  mutate(
    # standardize site label
    sample_site = str_squish(sample_site),
    sample_site = str_to_lower(sample_site),
    sample_site = str_replace_all(sample_site, "\\s+", "_"),

    # standardize type labels
    type = str_squish(type),

    # classify sampling category + depth
    sampling_class = case_when(
      type %in% c("A","B") ~ "SP",
      type %in% c("0-4","4-8") ~ "C",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ),
    depth = case_when(
      type == "0-4" ~ "0-4",
      type == "4-8" ~ "4-8",
      type %in% c("A","B") ~ NA_character_,
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ),

    # replicate id for SP
    sp_rep = case_when(
      type == "A" ~ "A",
      type == "B" ~ "B",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ),

    # composite depth label (useful later)
    comp_depth = case_when(
      sampling_class == "C" ~ depth,
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    )
  ) %>%
  # create a stable location id (site + sample site + coords)
  group_by(site, sample_site, lat, long) %>%
  mutate(location_id = cur_group_id()) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  # keep only relevant types
  filter(type %in% c("0-4","4-8","A","B")) %>%
  # make key factors ordered
  mutate(
    sampling_class = factor(sampling_class, levels = c("SP","C")),
    comp_depth = factor(comp_depth, levels = c("0-4","4-8")),
    sp_rep = factor(sp_rep, levels = c("A","B"))
  )

# Long format for plotting / modeling
lime_long <- lime_clean %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = all_of(nutrients), names_to = "nutrient", values_to = "value") %>%
  mutate(nutrient = factor(nutrient, levels = nutrients))

```

### Exploratory Plots

```{r}
#distribution curves of 0-4 comps vs 4-8 comps vs SP A and B (A and Bs can be together)
nutrients_all <- c("ca","k","mg","mn","p","zn","p_h_2","lbc_1","lb_ceq")
nutrients_to_log <- setdiff(nutrients_all, "p_h_2")

nutrient_names <- c(
  "ca"     = "Calcium (Ca)",
  "k"      = "Potassium (K)",
  "mg"     = "Magnesium (Mg)",
  "mn"     = "Manganese (Mn)",
  "p"      = "Phosphorus (P)",
  "zn"     = "Zinc (Zn)",
  "p_h_2"  = "pH",
  "lbc_1"  = "Lime Buffer Capacity (LBC)",
  "lb_ceq" = "Lime Buffer Capacity Equivalent (LBCeq)"
)

lime_plot <- lime_clean %>%
  # log1p everything except pH (this is the key change)
  mutate(across(all_of(nutrients_to_log), ~ log1p(.))) %>%
  pivot_longer(all_of(nutrients_all), names_to = "nutrient", values_to = "value") %>%
  mutate(
    sampling_type3 = case_when(
      type == "0-4" ~ "Composite 0–10 cm",
      type == "4-8" ~ "Composite 10-20 cm",
      type %in% c("A","B") ~ "Single Point (A+B)",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ),
    sampling_type3 = factor(
      sampling_type3,
      levels = c("Composite 0–10 cm", "Composite 10-20 cm", "Single Point (A+B)")
    ),
    nutrient = dplyr::recode(nutrient, !!!nutrient_names),
    nutrient = factor(nutrient, levels = unname(nutrient_names))
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(sampling_type3), !is.na(value))

p_den <- ggplot(lime_plot, aes(x = value, fill = sampling_type3)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.5) +
  facet_wrap(~ nutrient, scales = "free") +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(
      "Composite 0–10 cm" = "#1f77b4",
      "Composite 10-20 cm" = "#2ca02c",
      "Single Point (A+B)" = "#ff7f0e"
    ),
    na.translate = FALSE
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Value (log1p-transformed except pH)",
    y = "Density",
    title = "Density Plots of Bermuda Hay Grassland Soil Nutrients by Sampling Type",
    fill = "Sampling Type"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

p_den


ggsave("../output_do_not_push/nutrient_density_plots_by_sampling_type.png",
       p_den, width = 12, height = 8, dpi = 300, units = "in", limitsize = FALSE)

```

```{r}
nutrients_all <- c("ca","k","mg","mn","p","zn","p_h_2","lbc_1","lb_ceq")
nutrients_to_log <- setdiff(nutrients_all, "p_h_2")

lime_norm <- lime_clean %>%
  mutate(across(all_of(nutrients_to_log), ~ log1p(.))) %>%
  pivot_longer(all_of(nutrients_all), names_to = "nutrient", values_to = "value") %>%
  mutate(
    group = case_when(
      type == "0-4" ~ "Composite 0–4",
      type == "4-8" ~ "Composite 4–8",
      type == "A"   ~ "SP A",
      type == "B"   ~ "SP B",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ),
    group = factor(group,
                   levels = c("Composite 0–4","Composite 4–8","SP A","SP B"))
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(group), !is.na(value))
normality_3types <- lime_norm %>%
  group_by(nutrient, group) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    shapiro_p = ifelse(n >= 3,
                       shapiro.test(value)$p.value,
                       NA_real_),
    .groups = "drop"
  )
normality_wide <- normality_3types %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = group, values_from = shapiro_p)

normality_wide
normality_3types %>%
  group_by(group) %>%
  summarise(percent_normal = mean(shapiro_p > 0.05, na.rm = TRUE))
#none are normal. 0 out of 9 for almost all are abnormal
```

### Wilcoxon Rank Tests on Forage Sites

```{r}
#run wilcoxon rank tests on all comparison pairs for all nutrient with one table output per nutrient. rows would be the sites and bottom row would be combined sites, and columns would be the comparison pairs (SP A vs SP B, SP A vs C 0-4, SP A vs C 4-8, SP B vs C 0-4, SP B vs C 4-8, C 0-4 vs C 4-8)

# ---- helper: p-value -> stars ----
p_stars <- function(p) {
  case_when(
    is.na(p)        ~ "",
    p < 0.001       ~ "***",
    p < 0.01        ~ "**",
    p < 0.05        ~ "*",
    p < 0.1         ~ ".",
    TRUE            ~ ""
  )
}

# ---- build long table with group + transformed value ----
nutrients_all <- c("ca","k","mg","mn","p","zn","p_h_2","lbc_1","lb_ceq")

lime_wilcox <- lime_clean %>%
  pivot_longer(all_of(nutrients_all), names_to = "nutrient", values_to = "value_raw") %>%
  mutate(
    value = if_else(nutrient == "p_h_2", value_raw, log1p(value_raw)),
    group = case_when(
      type == "A"   ~ "SP A",
      type == "B"   ~ "SP B",
      type == "0-4" ~ "C 0–4",
      type == "4-8" ~ "C 4–8",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ),
    group = factor(group, levels = c("SP A","SP B","C 0–4","C 4–8"))
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(group), !is.na(value))

pairs <- list(
  c("SP A","SP B"),
  c("SP A","C 0–4"),
  c("SP A","C 4–8"),
  c("SP B","C 0–4"),
  c("SP B","C 4–8"),
  c("C 0–4","C 4–8")
)
pair_names <- c(
  "SP A vs SP B",
  "SP A vs C 0–4",
  "SP A vs C 4–8",
  "SP B vs C 0–4",
  "SP B vs C 4–8",
  "C 0–4 vs C 4–8"
)

run_wilcox_pairs <- function(df_site_nutrient) {
  map2_dfr(pairs, pair_names, function(pr, nm) {
    d <- df_site_nutrient %>%
      filter(group %in% pr) %>%
      select(location_id, group, value) %>%
      pivot_wider(names_from = group, values_from = value)

    x <- d[[pr[1]]]; y <- d[[pr[2]]]
    keep <- !is.na(x) & !is.na(y)
    x <- x[keep]; y <- y[keep]
    n_pairs <- length(x)

    if (n_pairs < 2) {
      return(tibble(comparison = nm, n_pairs = n_pairs, p_value = NA_real_))
    }

    wt <- wilcox.test(x, y, paired = TRUE, exact = FALSE)

    tibble(comparison = nm, n_pairs = n_pairs, p_value = wt$p.value)
  })
}

# per-site + all-sites
wilcox_results <- bind_rows(
  lime_wilcox %>%
    group_by(site, nutrient) %>%
    group_modify(~ run_wilcox_pairs(.x)) %>%
    ungroup() %>%
    mutate(site = as.character(site)),
  lime_wilcox %>%
    group_by(nutrient) %>%
    group_modify(~ run_wilcox_pairs(.x)) %>%
    ungroup() %>%
    mutate(site = "All sites")
) %>%
  mutate(
    stars = p_stars(p_value),
    p_fmt = if_else(is.na(p_value), "NA", formatC(p_value, format = "g", digits = 3)),
    cell = paste0(p_fmt, stars, " (n=", n_pairs, ")")
  )

# ---- split into separate nutrient tables ----
wilcox_tables_by_nutrient <- wilcox_results %>%
  select(nutrient, site, comparison, cell) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = comparison, values_from = cell) %>%
  arrange(site) %>%
  group_split(nutrient) %>%
  set_names(map_chr(group_split(wilcox_results, nutrient), ~ unique(.x$nutrient)))

# ---- print each table in HTML with a caption ----

walk(wilcox_tables_by_nutrient, ~{
  print(
    knitr::kable(.x %>% select(-nutrient),
                 caption = paste0("Wilcoxon paired tests — ", unique(.x$nutrient))) %>%
      kable_styling(
        bootstrap_options = c("striped"),
        full_width = FALSE,
        position = "center"
      )
  )
})

```

### Pull in Cropland Perry Data for Comparison

```{r}
#pull in perry data:
perry_surf <- read_csv("../data_do_not_push/perry_soil_points_surface.csv") %>%
  clean_names()

perry_fp <- read_csv("../data_do_not_push/Perry Full-Profile SampleData 1_15_2026_corrected.csv")  %>%
  clean_names() %>%
  #rename sample_point_id to Point_ID:
  rename(Point_ID = sample_id_lab)
perry_Ap_hz_results <- read_csv("../data_do_not_push/Ap_results_1_26_26.csv")  %>%
  clean_names()
perry_fp_locs <- read_csv("../data_do_not_push/Perry_full_profile_points.csv")  %>%
  clean_names()
#str(perry_fp_locs)
#add depths to dataset:

# str(depths)
# str(perry_fp)
# str(perry_Ap_hz_results)

# Parse Ap chemistry keys from "230045 - P1 - Ap"
perry_Ap_parsed <- perry_Ap_hz_results %>%
  mutate(sample = str_squish(sample)) %>%
  separate(sample, into = c("Point_ID", "point", "horizon_lab"),
           sep = "\\s*-\\s*", remove = FALSE, fill = "right", extra = "merge") %>%
  mutate(
    Point_ID = as.character(Point_ID) %>% str_trim(),
    point    = str_trim(point),
    horizon_lab = str_trim(horizon_lab)
  )

# Standardize perry_fp + parse depth range
perry_fp2 <- perry_fp %>%
  mutate(
    Point_ID = as.character(Point_ID) %>% str_trim(),
    point    = str_trim(point),
    horizon  = str_trim(horizon),
    to_depth_cm = str_squish(to_depth_cm)
  ) %>%
  separate(to_depth_cm, into = c("top_cm","bottom_cm"),
           sep = "\\s*-\\s*", remove = FALSE, fill = "right") %>%
  mutate(
    top_cm = parse_number(top_cm),
    bottom_cm = parse_number(bottom_cm)
  )

# Join chemistry (by sample ID lab)
perry_fp_with_chem <- perry_fp2 %>%
  left_join(
    perry_Ap_parsed %>%
      select(Point_ID, lab, lbc_1, lb_ceq, p_h_2, ca, k, mg, mn, p, zn),
    by = "Point_ID"
  )

# ---- Keep Ap / Ap1 / Ap2 / ^Ap ----
# This includes Ap2 now.
soil_vars <- c("lbc_1","lb_ceq","p_h_2","ca","k","mg","mn","p","zn")

perry_fp_Ap_family <- perry_fp_with_chem %>%
  # keep all Ap variants, including Ap2 and ^Ap
  filter(str_detect(horizon, "^(\\^)?Ap\\d*$")) %>%
  select(point, Point_ID, horizon,
         observational_method, potential_series_in_progress,
         to_depth_cm, top_cm, bottom_cm, h_depth_cm,
         all_of(soil_vars), lab)

# check what horizons you kept
perry_fp_Ap_family %>% count(horizon) %>% arrange(horizon)

```

### Cropland v. Forage Comparison

```{r}

### Surface-only comparison: Forage sites + Perry (SP vs COMP), with easy toggles
# Assumes you already have these objects from your earlier chunks:
#   - lime_clean   (forage dataset, cleaned with sampling_class, comp_depth, sp_rep, location_id, site, etc.)
#   - perry_surf   (Perry surface points with columns: type (SPID/CID), point_label, and nutrients)
# Also assumes:
#   nutrients_all / nutrients and p_stars() exist; if not, keep the definitions below.

# -----------------------------
# 0) Setup
# -----------------------------
nutrients_all <- c("ca","k","mg","mn","p","zn","p_h_2","lbc_1","lb_ceq")
nutrients_to_log <- setdiff(nutrients_all, "p_h_2")

p_stars <- function(p) {
  dplyr::case_when(
    is.na(p)  ~ "",
    p < 0.001 ~ "***",
    p < 0.01  ~ "**",
    p < 0.05  ~ "*",
    p < 0.1   ~ ".",
    TRUE      ~ "ns"
  )
}

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# 1) FORAGE surface: build one SP + one COMP per location_id
#    with toggles for choosing SP rep or COMP depth
# ---------------------------------------------------------

# ---- FORAGE SP toggle ----
# Default: average SP A and SP B per location_id
forage_sp <- lime_clean %>%
  filter(sampling_class == "SP", type %in% c("A","B")) %>%
  group_by(site, sample_site, lat, long, location_id) %>%
  summarise(across(all_of(nutrients_all), ~ mean(.x, na.rm = TRUE)), .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(sampling_type = "SP")

# OPTION: use only SP A (uncomment)
forage_sp <- lime_clean %>%
  filter(type == "A") %>%
  transmute(site, sample_site, lat, long, location_id,
            across(all_of(nutrients_all), as.numeric),
            sampling_type = "SP")

# OPTION: use only SP B (uncomment)
# forage_sp <- lime_clean %>%
#   filter(type == "B") %>%
#   transmute(site, sample_site, lat, long, location_id,
#             across(all_of(nutrients_all), as.numeric),
#             sampling_type = "SP")

# ---- FORAGE COMP toggle ----
# Default: average 0–4 and 4–8 per location_id
forage_comp <- lime_clean %>%
  filter(sampling_class == "C", type %in% c("0-4","4-8")) %>%
  group_by(site, sample_site, lat, long, location_id) %>%
  summarise(across(all_of(nutrients_all), ~ mean(.x, na.rm = TRUE)), .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(sampling_type = "COMP")

# OPTION: use only 0–4 (uncomment)
forage_comp <- lime_clean %>%
  filter(type == "0-4") %>%
  transmute(site, sample_site, lat, long, location_id,
            across(all_of(nutrients_all), as.numeric),
            sampling_type = "COMP")

# OPTION: use only 4–8 (uncomment)
# forage_comp <- lime_clean %>%
#   filter(type == "4-8") %>%
#   transmute(site, sample_site, lat, long, location_id,
#             across(all_of(nutrients_all), as.numeric),
#             sampling_type = "COMP")

forage_surface_wide <- bind_rows(forage_sp, forage_comp) %>%
  mutate(context = "Forage")

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# 2) PERRY surface: SPID vs CID as SP vs COMP (already surface)
# ---------------------------------------------------------
exclude_pts <- c("P1","P2","P3","P4","1","23","24","34","35","36","46","60","73")

perry_surface_wide <- perry_surf %>%
  clean_names() %>%
  mutate(
    PointLabel = as.character(point_label),
    type = as.character(type),
    sampling_type = case_when(
      type == "SPID" ~ "SP",
      type == "CID"  ~ "COMP",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    )
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(sampling_type)) %>%
  filter(!PointLabel %in% exclude_pts) %>%
  transmute(
    site = "Perry",
    context = "Perry",
    location_id = PointLabel,
    sampling_type,
    across(all_of(nutrients_all), as.numeric)
  )

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# 3) Combine Forage + Perry, long format w/ log1p transform
# ---------------------------------------------------------
# --- FIX: force location_id to character in BOTH datasets before bind_rows() ---
forage_surface_wide2 <- forage_surface_wide %>%
  mutate(
    site = as.character(site),
    context = as.character(context),
    sampling_type = as.character(sampling_type),
    location_id = as.character(location_id)
  ) %>%
  transmute(site, context, location_id, sampling_type, across(all_of(nutrients_all), as.numeric))

perry_surface_wide2 <- perry_surface_wide %>%
  mutate(
    site = as.character(site),
    context = as.character(context),
    sampling_type = as.character(sampling_type),
    location_id = as.character(location_id)
  ) %>%
  transmute(site, context, location_id, sampling_type, across(all_of(nutrients_all), as.numeric))

# --- Now this will work ---
surf_all_wide <- bind_rows(forage_surface_wide2, perry_surface_wide2)


surf_long <- surf_all_wide %>%
  pivot_longer(all_of(nutrients_all), names_to = "nutrient", values_to = "value_raw") %>%
  mutate(
    value = if_else(nutrient == "p_h_2", value_raw, log1p(value_raw)),
    nutrient = factor(nutrient, levels = nutrients_all),
    sampling_type = factor(sampling_type, levels = c("SP","COMP")),
    context = factor(context, levels = c("Forage","Perry"))
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(value))



# ---------------------------------------------------------
# 5) Wilcoxon tables (unpaired) + stars
#   - Forage vs Perry within SP and within COMP
#   - SP vs COMP within Forage and within Perry
# ---------------------------------------------------------

# helper for unpaired Wilcoxon
wilcox_unpaired <- function(df, group_var, g1, g2) {
  x <- df %>% filter(.data[[group_var]] == g1) %>% pull(value)
  y <- df %>% filter(.data[[group_var]] == g2) %>% pull(value)
  if (length(x) < 3 || length(y) < 3) return(NA_real_)
  wilcox.test(x, y, exact = FALSE)$p.value
}

# (1) Forage vs Perry within sampling_type
# ---- Perry vs Forage, within each nutrient x sampling_type (SP vs COMP) ----
# Assumes you have a long df like:
# surf_long: columns = nutrient, sampling_type (SP/COMP), context (Forage/Perry), value (already transformed if you want)

pvf_tbl <- surf_long %>%
  filter(context %in% c("Forage", "Perry")) %>%
  group_by(nutrient, sampling_type) %>%
  group_modify(~{
    d <- .x

    x <- d %>% filter(context == "Forage") %>% pull(value)
    y <- d %>% filter(context == "Perry")  %>% pull(value)

    x <- x[!is.na(x)]
    y <- y[!is.na(y)]

    n_forage <- length(x)
    n_perry  <- length(y)

    p_val <- if (n_forage >= 2 && n_perry >= 2) {
      wilcox.test(x, y, paired = FALSE, exact = FALSE)$p.value
    } else {
      NA_real_
    }

    tibble(
      n_forage = n_forage,
      n_perry  = n_perry,
      p_value  = p_val
    )
  }) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(
    sig = dplyr::case_when(
      is.na(p_value) ~ "",
      p_value < 0.001 ~ "***",
      p_value < 0.01  ~ "**",
      p_value < 0.05  ~ "*",
      p_value < 0.1   ~ ".",
      TRUE ~ "ns"
    )
  )

pvf_tbl


pvf_tbl_wide <- pvf_tbl %>%
  mutate(
    p_fmt = if_else(is.na(p_value), "NA", formatC(p_value, format="g", digits=3)),
    cell  = paste0(p_fmt, sig, " (nF=", n_forage, ", nP=", n_perry, ")")
  ) %>%
  select(nutrient, sampling_type, cell) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = sampling_type, values_from = cell)

pvf_tbl_wide






# =========================================================
# Helper: p-value annotations for "context" comparison
# within each nutrient x sampling_type (SP/COMP)
# =========================================================

p_stars2 <- function(p) {
  dplyr::case_when(
    is.na(p) ~ "",
    p < 0.001 ~ "***",
    p < 0.01  ~ "**",
    p < 0.05  ~ "*",
    p < 0.1   ~ ".",
    TRUE ~ "ns"
  )
}

make_context_pvals <- function(df_long, context_levels = c("Forage","Perry")) {
  df_long %>%
    filter(context %in% context_levels) %>%
    group_by(nutrient, sampling_type) %>%
    summarise(
      p_value = {
        d <- cur_data()
        x <- d %>% filter(context == context_levels[1]) %>% pull(value) %>% na.omit()
        y <- d %>% filter(context == context_levels[2]) %>% pull(value) %>% na.omit()
        if (length(x) < 3 || length(y) < 3) NA_real_ else wilcox.test(x, y, exact = FALSE)$p.value
      },
      y_max = max(value, na.rm = TRUE),
      y_min = min(value, na.rm = TRUE),
      .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    mutate(
      sig = p_stars2(p_value),
      p_fmt = if_else(is.na(p_value), "NA", formatC(p_value, format = "g", digits = 3)),
      label = paste0("p=", p_fmt, sig),
      # add a little separation so SP + COMP labels don't overlap
      y = y_max + 0.08 * (y_max - y_min) + if_else(as.character(sampling_type) == "COMP",
                                                   0.06 * (y_max - y_min), 0),
      x = 1.5  # midpoint between Forage (x=1) and Perry (x=2)
    )
}

plot_context_spcomp <- function(df_long, title_txt) {
  ann <- make_context_pvals(df_long)

  ggplot(df_long, aes(x = context, y = value, fill = sampling_type)) +
    geom_boxplot(
      width = 0.75,
      outlier.alpha = 0.25,
      position = position_dodge(width = 0.8)
    ) +
    geom_text(
      data = ann,
      aes(x = x, y = y, label = label, group = sampling_type),
      inherit.aes = FALSE,
      size = 3.2,
      fontface = "bold"
    ) +
    facet_wrap(~ nutrient, scales = "free_y", ncol = 3) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
    theme(
      strip.text = element_text(face = "bold"),
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 20, hjust = 1)
    ) +
    labs(
      x = NULL,
      y = "Value (log1p-transformed except pH)",
      title = title_txt,
      fill = "Sampling type"
    )
}

# =========================================================
# Plot 1) All forage vs Perry (your preferred layout + pvals)
# =========================================================
p_all <- plot_context_spcomp(
  surf_long,
  "Surface samples: cropland (Perry) vs forageland (All forage sites), with SP vs COMP side-by-side\n(p-values compare Forage vs Perry within SP and within COMP for each nutrient)"
)
print(p_all)

# =========================================================
# Plot 2) Coastal Plain subset: Tifton + Midville forage vs Perry
# (assumes surf_long has a 'site' column; it should, from your wide build)
# =========================================================
surf_long_cp <- surf_long %>%
  mutate(site = as.character(site)) %>%
  filter(
    context == "Perry" |
      (context == "Forage" & site %in% c("Tifton","Midville"))
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    context = factor(context, levels = c("Forage","Perry"))
  )

p_cp <- plot_context_spcomp(
  surf_long_cp,
  "Surface samples (Coastal Plain): cropland (Perry) vs forageland (Tifton + Midville), with SP vs COMP side-by-side\n(p-values compare Forage vs Perry within SP and within COMP for each nutrient)"
)
print(p_cp)

```



### Full-profile Ap (0–20 cm) vs Surface Comparison

```{r}
#remove points P1-P4, and 1,23,24,34,35,36,46,60,73)
perry_fp_Ap_family_clean <- perry_fp_Ap_family %>%
  filter(!point %in% c("P1","P2","P3","P4","1","23","24","34","35","36","46","60","73"))


#compare to surface data:

# ---------------------------------------
# 1) FP: keep only Ap horizons that represent 0–20 cm
#    - must start at 0
#    - must end <= 20
# ---------------------------------------
perry_fp_Ap_0_20 <- perry_fp_Ap_family_clean %>%
  filter(!is.na(top_cm), !is.na(bottom_cm)) %>%
  filter(top_cm == 0, bottom_cm <= 20) %>%
  mutate(dataset = "FP Ap (0–20 cm)") %>%
  # keep point as the "label" to match surface "PointLabel"
  transmute(
    dataset,
    PointLabel = point,
    horizon,
    top_cm, bottom_cm,
    across(all_of(soil_vars), as.numeric)
  )

# sanity check
perry_fp_Ap_0_20 %>% count(horizon) %>% arrange(horizon)

# ---------------------------------------
# 2) Surface: keep SPID + CID, remove excluded points
# ---------------------------------------
exclude_pts <- c("P1","P2","P3","P4","1","23","24","34","35","36","46","60","73")

perry_surf_clean <- perry_surf %>%
  mutate(
    PointLabel = as.character(point_label),
    type = as.character(type)
  ) %>%
  filter(type %in% c("SPID","CID")) %>%
  filter(!PointLabel %in% exclude_pts) %>%
  mutate(dataset = paste0("Surface ", type)) %>%
  transmute(
    dataset,
    PointLabel,
    type,
    across(all_of(soil_vars), as.numeric)
  )

# ---------------------------------------
# 3) Combine to long format for plotting/testing
# ---------------------------------------
plot_df <- bind_rows(
  perry_surf_clean %>% select(dataset, PointLabel, all_of(soil_vars)),
  perry_fp_Ap_0_20 %>% select(dataset, PointLabel, all_of(soil_vars))
) %>%
  pivot_longer(all_of(soil_vars), names_to = "nutrient", values_to = "value") %>%
  filter(!is.na(value)) %>%
  mutate(
    nutrient = factor(nutrient, levels = soil_vars),
    dataset  = factor(dataset, levels = c("Surface SPID", "Surface CID", "FP Ap (0–20 cm)")),
    value_plot = case_when(
      nutrient == "p_h_2" ~ value,
      value > 0 ~ log10(value),
      TRUE ~ NA_real_
    )
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(value_plot))

# ---------------------------------------
# 4) Plot: 3 boxplots per nutrient (SPID vs CID vs FP)
# ---------------------------------------
ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = dataset, y = value_plot, fill = dataset)) +
  geom_boxplot(width = 0.75, outlier.alpha = 0.25) +
  facet_wrap(~ nutrient, scales = "free_y", ncol = 3) +
  labs(
    x = NULL, y = NULL,
    title = "Surface (SPID, CID) vs Full-Profile Ap horizons constrained to 0–20 cm (log10 except pH)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 25, hjust = 1)
  )

wilcox_tbl_perry <- plot_df %>%
  filter(dataset %in% c("Surface SPID","Surface CID","FP Ap (0–20 cm)")) %>%
  group_by(nutrient) %>%
  summarise(
    p_spid = wilcox.test(
      value_plot[dataset=="FP Ap (0–20 cm)"],
      value_plot[dataset=="Surface SPID"],
      exact = FALSE
    )$p.value,

    p_cid = wilcox.test(
      value_plot[dataset=="FP Ap (0–20 cm)"],
      value_plot[dataset=="Surface CID"],
      exact = FALSE
    )$p.value,

    p_spid_cid = wilcox.test(
      value_plot[dataset=="Surface SPID"],
      value_plot[dataset=="Surface CID"],
      exact = FALSE
    )$p.value,

    .groups = "drop"
  )
wilcox_tbl_stars <- wilcox_tbl_perry %>%
  mutate(
    sig_fp_spid = case_when(
      p_spid < 0.001 ~ "***",
      p_spid < 0.01  ~ "**",
      p_spid < 0.05  ~ "*",
      TRUE           ~ "ns"
    ),
    sig_fp_cid = case_when(
      p_cid < 0.001 ~ "***",
      p_cid < 0.01  ~ "**",
      p_cid < 0.05  ~ "*",
      TRUE          ~ "ns"
    ),
    sig_spid_cid = case_when(
      p_spid_cid < 0.001 ~ "***",
      p_spid_cid < 0.01  ~ "**",
      p_spid_cid < 0.05  ~ "*",
      TRUE               ~ "ns"
    )
  )

wilcox_tbl_stars
```

#local correlation:
```{r}

#----------------------------
# 0) Build clean coordinate keys (NO many-to-many)
#----------------------------

# Surface coords keyed by PointLabel + type (SPID/CID)
surf_xy_key <- perry_surf %>%
  transmute(
    PointLabel = as.character(point_label),
    type = as.character(type),
    x = as.numeric(x),
    y = as.numeric(y)
  ) %>%
  distinct(PointLabel, type, .keep_all = TRUE)

# FP coords keyed by PointLabel (P1, P2, ...)
fp_xy_key <- perry_fp_locs %>%
  transmute(
    PointLabel = as.character(point_id_2),
    x = as.numeric(point_x),
    y = as.numeric(point_y)
  ) %>%
  distinct(PointLabel, .keep_all = TRUE)

#----------------------------
# 1) Surface sf (SPID + CID)  -> dataset = SPID/CID
#----------------------------
surf_sf <- perry_surf_clean %>%
  mutate(
    PointLabel = as.character(PointLabel),
    type = as.character(type)
  ) %>%
  left_join(surf_xy_key, by = c("PointLabel","type")) %>%
  filter(!is.na(x), !is.na(y)) %>%
  st_as_sf(coords = c("x","y"), crs = 4326, remove = FALSE) %>%
  st_transform(26917) %>%
  mutate(dataset = type)   # <- IMPORTANT: dataset becomes SPID or CID

#----------------------------
# 2) FP sf (0–20cm Ap family) -> dataset = FP
#----------------------------
fp_sf <- perry_fp_Ap_0_20 %>%
  mutate(PointLabel = as.character(PointLabel)) %>%
  left_join(fp_xy_key, by = "PointLabel") %>%
  filter(!is.na(x), !is.na(y)) %>%
  st_as_sf(coords = c("x","y"), crs = 3857, remove = FALSE) %>%  # FP coords are Web Mercator meters
  st_transform(26917) %>%
  mutate(dataset = "FP")


#save dataset:
# write_csv(fp_sf %>%
#             st_drop_geometry(),
#           "../data_do_not_push/perry_fp_Ap_0_20_with_coords.csv")

#----------------------------
# 3) Combine + sanity check
#----------------------------
all_sf <- bind_rows(surf_sf, fp_sf) %>%
  mutate(dataset = factor(dataset, levels = c("SPID","CID","FP")))

cat("Counts by dataset:\n")
print(all_sf %>% st_drop_geometry() %>% count(dataset))

#----------------------------
# 4) Distance-decay helper (pairs among NON-NA values)
#----------------------------
distance_decay <- function(sfobj, var){
  v <- sfobj[[var]]
  idx <- which(!is.na(v))

  if (length(idx) < 2) {
    return(tibble(i = integer(), j = integer(), dist = numeric(), diff = numeric()))
  }

  # compute distances only for idx points (smaller + avoids irrelevant NA rows)
  dmat <- st_distance(sfobj[idx,], sfobj[idx,])
  dmat <- units::drop_units(dmat)

  pairs <- combn(seq_along(idx), 2)  # indices inside idx vector

  tibble(
    i = idx[pairs[1, ]],
    j = idx[pairs[2, ]],
    dist = dmat[cbind(pairs[1, ], pairs[2, ])],
    diff = abs(v[idx[pairs[1, ]]] - v[idx[pairs[2, ]]])
  )
}

#----------------------------
# 5) Build long decay data for all nutrients
#----------------------------
decay_long <- map_dfr(soil_vars, \(nm){
  df <- distance_decay(all_sf, nm)
  df$nutrient <- nm
  df
})

cat("Rows in decay_long:", nrow(decay_long), "\n")

#----------------------------
# 6) Binned pooled plot (ALL points)
#----------------------------
decay_binned <- decay_long %>%
  filter(is.finite(dist), is.finite(diff)) %>%
  group_by(nutrient) %>%
  mutate(
    dist_bin = cut(dist,
                   breaks = seq(0, max(dist, na.rm = TRUE), by = 50),
                   include.lowest = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  group_by(nutrient, dist_bin) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_dist = mean(dist, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_diff = mean(diff, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

p_pooled <- ggplot(decay_binned, aes(x = mean_dist, y = mean_diff)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", span = 0.6, se = FALSE) +
  facet_wrap(~ nutrient, scales = "free_y", ncol = 3) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  labs(
    x = "Distance between points (m)",
    y = "Mean absolute difference",
    title = "Distance–similarity decay of Ap Horizon Samples (0-20 cm) with Single Point and Composite Samples (0-15 cm)"
  )
print(p_pooled)

#----------------------------
# 7) Pair-type labeling (SPID–SPID, CID–CID, FP–FP, SPID–CID, etc.)
#----------------------------
ds_vec <- as.character(all_sf$dataset)

decay_long2 <- decay_long %>%
  mutate(
    ds_i = ds_vec[i],
    ds_j = ds_vec[j],
    pair_type = if_else(ds_i == ds_j,
                        paste0(ds_i, "–", ds_j),
                        paste0(pmin(ds_i, ds_j), "–", pmax(ds_i, ds_j)))
  )

cat("Pair type counts:\n")
print(decay_long2 %>% count(pair_type))

#----------------------------
# 8) SPID-only + CID-only plot sets (raw scatter + loess)
#----------------------------
# spid_df <- decay_long2 %>% filter(pair_type == "SPID–SPID")
# cid_df  <- decay_long2 %>% filter(pair_type == "CID–CID")
# 
# if (nrow(spid_df) > 0) {
#   p_spid <- ggplot(spid_df, aes(x = dist, y = diff)) +
#     geom_point(alpha = 0.2, size = 0.8) +
#     geom_smooth(se = FALSE, method = "loess", span = 0.6) +
#     facet_wrap(~ nutrient, scales = "free_y", ncol = 3) +
#     labs(x = "Distance (m)", y = "Absolute difference |Δ|",
#          title = "Distance–dissimilarity decay (SPID only)") +
#     theme_minimal(base_size = 12)
#   print(p_spid)
# } else {
#   message("No SPID–SPID pairs found. Check dataset labels and that SPID values exist for these nutrients.")
# }
# 
# if (nrow(cid_df) > 0) {
#   p_cid <- ggplot(cid_df, aes(x = dist, y = diff)) +
#     geom_point(alpha = 0.2, size = 0.8) +
#     geom_smooth(se = FALSE, method = "loess", span = 0.6) +
#     facet_wrap(~ nutrient, scales = "free_y", ncol = 3) +
#     labs(x = "Distance (m)", y = "Absolute difference |Δ|",
#          title = "Distance–dissimilarity decay (CID only)") +
#     theme_minimal(base_size = 12)
#   print(p_cid)
# } else {
#   message("No CID–CID pairs found. Check dataset labels and that CID values exist for these nutrients.")
# }

# ----------------------------
# Binned decay helper (same style as pooled)
# ----------------------------
bin_decay <- function(df, bin_width = 50){
  df %>%
    filter(is.finite(dist), is.finite(diff)) %>%
    group_by(nutrient) %>%
    mutate(
      dist_bin = cut(
        dist,
        breaks = seq(0, max(dist, na.rm = TRUE), by = bin_width),
        include.lowest = TRUE
      )
    ) %>%
    ungroup() %>%
    group_by(nutrient, dist_bin) %>%
    summarise(
      mean_dist = mean(dist, na.rm = TRUE),
      mean_diff = mean(diff, na.rm = TRUE),
      .groups = "drop"
    )
}

bin_width <- 50  # same as pooled

# ----------------------------
# SPID-only binned plot (same style as pooled)
# ----------------------------
spid_binned <- decay_long2 %>%
  filter(pair_type == "SPID–SPID") %>%
  bin_decay(bin_width = bin_width)

ggplot(spid_binned, aes(x = mean_dist, y = mean_diff)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", span = 0.6, se = FALSE) +
  facet_wrap(~ nutrient, scales = "free_y", ncol = 3) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  labs(
    x = "Distance between points (m)",
    y = "Mean absolute difference",
    title = "Distance–similarity decay of Single Point Samples (0-15 cm)"
  )

# ----------------------------
# CID-only binned plot (same style as pooled)
# ----------------------------
cid_binned <- decay_long2 %>%
  filter(pair_type == "CID–CID") %>%
  bin_decay(bin_width = bin_width)

ggplot(cid_binned, aes(x = mean_dist, y = mean_diff)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", span = 0.6, se = FALSE) +
  facet_wrap(~ nutrient, scales = "free_y", ncol = 3) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  labs(
    x = "Distance between points (m)",
    y = "Mean absolute difference",
    title = "Distance–similarity decay of Composite Samples (0-15 cm)"
  )
```


### Variogram stuff (no correlation big picture:)

```{r}

soil_vars <- c("lbc_1","lb_ceq","p_h_2","ca","k","mg","mn","p","zn")

#--- 0) Ensure key columns exist (quick sanity)
names(perry_fp_locs)

# perry_fp_locs has point_id_2 (P1, P2, ...) and point_x/point_y in EPSG:3857
fp_locs_key <- perry_fp_locs %>%
  transmute(
    point = str_trim(point_id_2),  # "P1", "P2", ...
    point_x = as.numeric(point_x),
    point_y = as.numeric(point_y),
    point_id = point_id,           # keep if you want
    notes = notes
  ) %>%
  distinct(point, .keep_all = TRUE)

#--- 1) Parse Ap chemistry keys from "230045 - P1 - Ap"
perry_Ap_parsed <- perry_Ap_hz_results %>%
  mutate(sample = str_squish(sample)) %>%
  separate(
    sample,
    into = c("Point_ID", "point", "horizon_lab"),
    sep = "\\s*-\\s*",
    remove = FALSE,
    fill = "right",
    extra = "merge"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    Point_ID = as.character(Point_ID) %>% str_trim(),
    point = str_trim(point),
    horizon_lab = str_trim(horizon_lab)
  )

#--- 2) Standardize perry_fp and parse depth range
perry_fp2 <- perry_fp %>%
  mutate(
    Point_ID = as.character(Point_ID) %>% str_trim(),
    point    = str_trim(point),
    horizon  = str_trim(horizon),
    to_depth_cm = str_squish(to_depth_cm)
  ) %>%
  separate(
    to_depth_cm,
    into = c("top_cm","bottom_cm"),
    sep = "\\s*-\\s*",
    remove = FALSE,
    fill = "right"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    top_cm = parse_number(top_cm),
    bottom_cm = parse_number(bottom_cm)
  )

#--- 3) Join Ap chemistry onto profile rows (by sample id lab = Point_ID)
perry_fp_with_chem <- perry_fp2 %>%
  left_join(
    perry_Ap_parsed %>%
      select(Point_ID, lab, all_of(soil_vars)),
    by = "Point_ID"
  )

#--- 4) Join coords onto *every* profile row using point label (P1, P2, ...)
perry_fp_merged <- perry_fp_with_chem %>%
  left_join(fp_locs_key, by = "point") %>%
  relocate(point, Point_ID, horizon, to_depth_cm, top_cm, bottom_cm, point_x, point_y, .after = 1)

#--- 5) Sanity checks (these should NOT be 0)
cat("Profile rows:", nrow(perry_fp_with_chem), "\n")
cat("Merged rows :", nrow(perry_fp_merged), "\n")
cat("Rows with coords:", sum(!is.na(perry_fp_merged$point_x) & !is.na(perry_fp_merged$point_y)), "\n")

# -----------------------------
# 1) FP: keep Ap-family only, restrict to 0–20, then ONE row per profile (point)
#    - must start at 0
#    - bottom <= 20
#    - if multiple (Ap, Ap1, Ap2, ^Ap), keep deepest bottom_cm within 0–20
# -----------------------------
fp_0_20_one <- perry_fp_merged %>%
  filter(!is.na(point_x), !is.na(point_y)) %>%
  mutate(horizon = str_trim(horizon)) %>%
  filter(str_detect(horizon, "^(\\^)?Ap\\d*$")) %>%                 # Ap family
  filter(!is.na(top_cm), !is.na(bottom_cm)) %>%
  filter(top_cm == 0, bottom_cm <= 20) %>%                         # 0–20 only
  group_by(point) %>%
  arrange(desc(bottom_cm), horizon) %>%                            # prefer deepest within 0–20
  slice(1) %>%                                                     # ONE per point/profile
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(dataset = "FP") %>%
  select(dataset, point, point_x, point_y, all_of(soil_vars))

# -----------------------------
# 2) Make SF objects in common CRS (UTM 17N meters)
#    - Surface x/y are lon/lat -> EPSG:4326
#    - FP point_x/point_y are Web Mercator meters -> EPSG:3857
# -----------------------------
surf_sf <- st_as_sf(perry_surf, coords = c("x","y"), crs = 4326, remove = FALSE) %>%
  st_transform(26917) %>%                # UTM 17N meters
  mutate(dataset = type) %>%             # SPID / CID
  select(dataset, all_of(soil_vars), geometry)

fp_sf <- st_as_sf(fp_0_20_one, coords = c("point_x","point_y"), crs = 3857, remove = FALSE) %>%
  st_transform(st_crs(surf_sf)) %>%      # match UTM 17N
  select(dataset, all_of(soil_vars), geometry)

all_sf <- bind_rows(surf_sf, fp_sf) %>%
  mutate(dataset = factor(dataset, levels = c("SPID","CID","FP")))

# -----------------------------
# 3) Helper: build variogram DF for one nutrient:
#    - pooled (ALL)
#    - SPID-only
#    - CID-only
#    - FP-only
# -----------------------------
make_variogram_df <- function(sf_all, var, width = 25, cutoff = NULL, min_n = 15) {
  dat_all <- sf_all %>% select(dataset, !!sym(var)) %>% filter(!is.na(.data[[var]]))

  if (nrow(dat_all) < min_n) return(NULL)

  # default cutoff: half the diagonal of bounding box
  if (is.null(cutoff)) {
    bb <- st_bbox(dat_all)
    maxd <- sqrt((bb$xmax - bb$xmin)^2 + (bb$ymax - bb$ymin)^2)
    cutoff <- as.numeric(maxd) / 2
  }

  # pooled
  vg_all <- gstat::variogram(as.formula(paste0(var, " ~ 1")),
                             data = dat_all, cutoff = cutoff, width = width) %>%
    mutate(group = "ALL (pooled)", nutrient = var)

  # by dataset
  vg_by <- levels(sf_all$dataset) %>%
    map_dfr(function(ds) {
      dat <- sf_all %>% filter(dataset == ds) %>% select(dataset, !!sym(var)) %>% filter(!is.na(.data[[var]]))
      if (nrow(dat) < min_n) return(NULL)
      gstat::variogram(as.formula(paste0(var, " ~ 1")),
                       data = dat, cutoff = cutoff, width = width) %>%
        mutate(group = paste0(ds, " only"), nutrient = var)
    })

  bind_rows(vg_all, vg_by)
}

# -----------------------------
# 4) Compute variograms for all nutrients + plot
# -----------------------------
width_m <- 25    # lag bin width (m). Try 25, 50, 75
cutoff_m <- NULL # set a number if you want fixed cutoff, else auto
min_n <- 15

vg_long <- map_dfr(soil_vars, ~ make_variogram_df(all_sf, .x, width = width_m, cutoff = cutoff_m, min_n = min_n))

# Print a quick summary of sample sizes per nutrient/type (helps debug)
sizes_tbl <- map_dfr(soil_vars, function(v) {
  all_sf %>%
    st_drop_geometry() %>%
    mutate(v = .data[[v]]) %>%
    group_by(dataset) %>%
    summarise(n = sum(!is.na(v)), .groups = "drop") %>%
    mutate(nutrient = v) %>%
    relocate(nutrient)
})
print(sizes_tbl)

# Variogram plot grid
p_vg <- ggplot(vg_long, aes(x = dist, y = gamma, color = group)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.85) +
  geom_line(alpha = 0.75) +
  facet_wrap(~ nutrient, scales = "free_y", ncol = 3) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  labs(
    x = "Lag distance (m)",
    y = "Semivariance",
    title = "Semivariograms by nutrient: pooled vs within sampling type (SPID, CID, FP 0–20 one-per-profile)"
  )
print(p_vg)


```
```{r}
vg_fits <- vg_long %>%
  group_by(nutrient, group) %>%
  group_modify(~{
    emp <- .x

    # If too few points → return empty tibble
    if (nrow(emp) < 5) {
      return(tibble(
        model = character(),
        nugget = numeric(),
        sill = numeric(),
        range_m = numeric(),
        sse = numeric(),
        fit_obj = list()
      ))
    }

    sill0  <- max(emp$gamma, na.rm = TRUE)
    range0 <- max(emp$dist, na.rm = TRUE) / 3

    models <- c("Sph","Exp","Gau")

    fits <- map_dfr(models, function(m){
      v0 <- vgm(psill = sill0, model = m, range = range0)
      fit <- tryCatch(fit.variogram(emp, v0), error = function(e) NULL)

      if (is.null(fit)) return(NULL)

      tibble(
        model = m,
        nugget = ifelse(any(fit$model=="Nug"),
                        fit$psill[fit$model=="Nug"], 0),
        sill   = sum(fit$psill[fit$model!="Nug"]),
        range_m = fit$range[fit$model!="Nug"][1],
        sse = attr(fit,"SSErr"),
        fit_obj = list(fit)
      )
    })

    # If no model fit → return empty tibble
    if (nrow(fits) == 0) {
      return(tibble(
        model = character(),
        nugget = numeric(),
        sill = numeric(),
        range_m = numeric(),
        sse = numeric(),
        fit_obj = list()
      ))
    }

    fits[which.min(fits$sse), ]
  }) %>%
  ungroup()
vg_fits
```
```{r}
# FP-only SF object from previous step
fp_only <- all_sf %>% 
  filter(dataset == "FP")

fit_variogram_fp <- function(var, width = 25) {

  dat <- fp_only %>%
    select(!!sym(var)) %>%
    filter(!is.na(.data[[var]]))

  if (nrow(dat) < 15) return(NULL)

  # empirical variogram
  v_emp <- variogram(as.formula(paste0(var, " ~ 1")), dat, width = width)

  # initial guesses
  vgm0 <- vgm(psill = var(dat[[var]], na.rm = TRUE),
              model = "Exp",
              range = max(v_emp$dist)/3,
              nugget = 0)

  # try several models
  fits <- list(
    Exp = fit.variogram(v_emp, vgm("Exp")),
    Sph = fit.variogram(v_emp, vgm("Sph")),
    Gau = fit.variogram(v_emp, vgm("Gau"))
  )

  # compute SSE
  sse <- map_dbl(fits, ~ attr(.x, "SSErr"))
  best <- names(which.min(sse))
  best_fit <- fits[[best]]

  list(
    nutrient = var,
    emp = v_emp,
    fit = best_fit,
    model = best,
    nugget = best_fit$psill[1],
    sill = sum(best_fit$psill),
    range = best_fit$range[2],
    sse = min(sse)
  )
}

# Fit all nutrients
fp_fits <- map(soil_vars, fit_variogram_fp)

# Summary table
fp_summary <- map_dfr(fp_fits, ~{
  if (is.null(.x)) return(NULL)
  tibble(
    nutrient = .x$nutrient,
    model = .x$model,
    nugget = round(.x$nugget, 3),
    sill = round(.x$sill, 3),
    nugget_ratio = round(.x$nugget / .x$sill, 3),
    range_m = round(.x$range, 1),
    sse = round(.x$sse, 2)
  )
})

fp_summary
vg_plots_fp <- map(fp_fits, function(res) {
  if (is.null(res)) return(NULL)

  ggplot(res$emp, aes(dist, gamma)) +
    geom_point() +
    geom_line(data = variogramLine(res$fit, maxdist = max(res$emp$dist)),
              aes(dist, gamma), color = "red", linewidth = 1) +
    theme_minimal() +
    labs(
      title = paste(res$nutrient, "| FP-only |", res$model),
      subtitle = paste("range =", round(res$range,1), "m | nugget ratio =", round(res$nugget/res$sill,2)),
      x = "Distance (m)",
      y = "Semivariance"
    )
})

# show them
wrap_plots(vg_plots_fp)

```
